title: Stasi Museum Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **The Stasi Museum sits inside the real building: House 1 of the former Ministry for State Security on Ruschestraße in Lichtenberg.** You walk through Erich Mielke's preserved office suite, the surveillance gadgets, disguises and hidden cameras, and the ministry's own rooms. U-Bahn Magdalenenstraße on the U5, about 15 minutes from Alexanderplatz.

**Tickets in 2026: 12 euros adult, 9 reduced. Open Monday to Friday 10:00 to 18:00, weekends and holidays 11:00 to 18:00.** It is self-guided; an English guided tour runs at 15:00 on weekdays for 5 euros extra.

**The heavier, more moving site is the Stasi prison, not the museum.** Gedenkstätte Berlin-Hohenschönhausen, the Stasi's main remand prison, is a separate place at Genslerstraße 66 (tram M5). You can only see the cells on a guided tour, and many tours are led by former prisoners.
**Hohenschönhausen: open daily 10:00 to 18:00, tour about 9 euros, English tours several times a day.** Book online in advance, because you cannot walk the cell blocks on your own.

**Why it matters: by 1989 the Stasi had 91,015 full-time officers and roughly 189,000 unofficial informers,** and it held files on about a third of East Germany's population. Both sites document that machine.
**On a short trip, do one, not both.** The museum is quicker and closer to the centre; the prison is further out, heavier, and needs a booked tour slot. Pair either with a wider Cold War Berlin day.
